skill.name: zentao-task-planner
skill.description: Plan Zentao tasks, and perform Zentao operations including creating tasks, logging work hours, and completing tasks.
skill.summary: Claude-powered assistant for planning, creating, and managing Zentao project management tasks with work hour tracking
skill.author: AriesYB

security_audit.summary: Scanned 17 files (1770 lines). Static analyzer flagged 173 patterns, but extensive manual review confirms this is a legitimate Zentao task management skill with mostly false positive detections. The skill operates on user-configured self-hosted Zentao instances only, uses environment variables for credentials (standard pattern), and all modifications require explicit user confirmation with --execute flag. No malicious intent or exfiltration behavior detected.

medium_findings.title: Hardcoded Zentao URL Pattern in Documentation
medium_findings.description: Documentation files reference example Zentao URLs (https://your-zentao-instance.com/zentao/). These are placeholder examples, not actual connections. The skill requires users to configure their own Zentao instance URL in .env file.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documentation clearly shows placeholder URLs with 'your-zentao-instance.com' - standard documentation pattern, not credential exposure

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for Credential Management
medium_findings.description: Scripts access environment variables (ZENTAO_BASE_URL, ZENTAO_ACCOUNT, ZENTAO_PASSWORD) to authenticate with user's own Zentao instance. This is a standard and expected pattern for configuration-based credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: ZentaoConfig.from_env() properly validates required credentials before use - legitimate configuration management

low_findings.title: Python HTTP Requests Library Usage
low_findings.description: Uses requests library for HTTP communication with Zentao API. Standard Python networking library with no malicious capabilities.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: requests library is a standard Python HTTP client - no malicious networking patterns

low_findings.title: Bash Command Examples in Markdown Documentation
low_findings.description: Documentation files contain Bash commands prefixed with `$` in markdown code blocks. These are user-facing examples for documentation, not actual shell execution in the skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown documentation with code blocks - legitimate documentation format, not executable code

low_findings.title: Python dotenv Library for Configuration Loading
low_findings.description: Uses python-dotenv to load configuration from .env files. Standard configuration pattern for storing credentials locally.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: dotenv is a standard Python configuration library - legitimate use for local credential storage

content.value_statement: Project managers and developers waste hours on repetitive Zentao task management. This skill enables Claude to plan tasks, create batch operations, and track work hours directly in your Zentao instance.
actual_capabilities: Query next consecutive workdays with Chinese calendar support
List tasks from multiple views (active, assigned, finished, closed)
Create tasks in batch from standardized TSV/tab-separated input
Complete tasks by date range with dry-run preview before execution
Close finished tasks that are still open
Retrieve task type enumerations from your Zentao instance
limitations: Requires Zentao instance with valid credentials configured in .env file
Cannot create tasks without first confirming plan with user
Only operates on Zentao instances accessible from the execution environment

use_cases.title: Weekly Task Planning Session
use_cases.description: Project managers use Claude to plan the week's tasks by querying workdays, then batch-creating tasks from a prepared spreadsheet.

use_cases.title: Automated Task Completion
use_cases.description: Developers automatically complete tasks that have passed their deadline by running the finish-tasks-by-date script with preview confirmation.

use_cases.title: Work Hour Reporting
use_cases.description: Team members query their assigned tasks and log work progress directly through Claude, maintaining accurate time tracking.

prompt_templates.title: Plan this week's tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the zentao-task-planner skill to plan tasks for this week. Show me the upcoming workdays first.

prompt_templates.title: Create multiple tasks from a table
prompt_templates.prompt: Create tasks in Zentao using this task plan: [paste TSV data]. Preview first, then create after confirmation.

prompt_templates.title: Close all finished tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Preview which tasks are finished but not closed in Zentao, then close them with comment 'Batch closed by Claude'.

prompt_templates.title: Complete overdue tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Find tasks with deadline before today and auto-complete them. Run in preview mode first.

output_examples.input: Show me my active tasks in Zentao
output_examples.output:
Found 5 active tasks:
1. Implement user authentication - Priority 1, Due 2026-05-10
2. Fix login bug - Priority 2, Due 2026-05-08
3. Write unit tests - Priority 3, Due 2026-05-12

Run with --summary-only for compact view or --output tsv for spreadsheet export.

Upcoming workdays:
- 2026-05-07 (Thursday)
- 2026-05-08 (Friday)
- 2026-05-11 (Monday)

Weekend (May 9-10) excluded. Today is not a workday.

best_practices: Always preview changes with default dry-run mode before using --execute flag
Store your .env file in the skill directory and never commit it to version control
Use --env-file to specify custom environment files for different Zentao instances
anti_patterns: Do not run --execute without reviewing the preview output first
Do not share your ZENTAO_PASSWORD in public repositories or conversations
Do not skip user confirmation when creating or closing multiple tasks

faq.question: How do I configure my Zentao credentials?
faq.answer: Create a .env file with ZENTAO_BASE_URL, ZENTAO_ACCOUNT, ZENTAO_PASSWORD, and ZENTAO_NAME. Place it in the skill directory or use --env-file to specify its location.

faq.question: What happens if I run a script without --execute?
faq.answer: Scripts run in preview mode by default. You will see what would happen without making actual changes. Add --execute only after reviewing the preview.

faq.question: Can I use this with a self-hosted Zentao?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill works with any Zentao instance that has API access enabled. Configure the URL in ZENTAO_BASE_URL.

faq.question: How do I batch create tasks?
faq.answer: Prepare your tasks as a tab-separated (TSV) file and use create_tasks.py with --plan-file. The script validates input before creation.

faq.question: Why are no tasks created even though I ran the script?
faq.answer: Check that you added --execute flag. Without it, the script only validates and shows what would be created.

faq.question: Does this skill support Chinese calendar holidays?
faq.answer: Yes. The get_next_workdays script uses the chinese-calendar library to skip weekends and official holidays.
